results.raw_fol:
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
all x (akmi(x) -> -exists y (parakmi(y) & before(y,x))) -> exists x akmi(x) 
becomes 
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
exists x akmi(x) -> all x (akmi(x) -> -exists y (parakmi(y) & before(y,x)))
However the best representation is 
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
all x (akmi(x) -> forall y (parakmi(y) -> after(x,y)))
Rewritten as 
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
all x (akmi(x) -> -exists y (parakmi(y) & -after(x,y)))
Then to 
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
all x (akmi(x) -> ~exists y (parakmi(y) & before(y,x)))
Rewritten in Prover9 syntax as:
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
all x (akmi(x) -> -exists y (parakmi(y) & before(y,x)))

So the final output is 
Premises:
all x (akmi(x) -> exists y (parakmi(y) & after(x,y)))
Hypothesis:
all x (akmi(x) -> -exists y (parakmi(y) & before(y,x)))

results.raw_fol:
Premises:
blows & -rains
Hypothesis:
-(blows & -rains)

should be rewritten as:

Premises:
blows & -rains
Hypothesis:
rains & -blows

Using Prover9 syntax and the given translation conventions, we get:

Premises:
blows & -rains
Hypothesis:
rains & -blows

Let's directly translate the given sentences:
"Φυσάει και δε βρέχει" translates to "It is windy and it is not raining" or simply "blows & -rains".
"Βρέχει και δε φυσάει" translates to "It is raining and it is not windy" or "rains & -blows".

Thus, 
Premises:
blows & -rains
Hypothesis:
rains & -blows
